Submodules

cns.pipelines module

cns.pipelines.main_aggregate(cns_df, segs, how='mean', cn_columns=None, print_info=False)
cns.pipelines.main_coverage(cns_df, samples_df=None, cn_columns=None, segs=None, assembly=<cns.utils.assemblies.Assembly object>, print_info=False)
cns.pipelines.main_fill(cns_df, samples_df=None, cn_columns=None, assembly=<cns.utils.assemblies.Assembly object>, add_missing_chromosomes=True, print_info=False)
cns.pipelines.main_impute(cns_df, samples_df=None, method='extend', cn_columns=None, print_info=False)
cns.pipelines.main_ploidy(cns_df, samples_df=None, cn_columns=None, segs=None, assembly=<cns.utils.assemblies.Assembly object>, print_info=False)
cns.pipelines.main_segment(cns_df, select_segs=None, remove_segs=None, split_size=0, merge_dist=0, filter_size=0, assembly=<cns.utils.assemblies.Assembly object>, print_info=False)
cns.pipelines.main_signatures(cns_df, samples_df=None, cn_columns=None, segs=None, assembly=<cns.utils.assemblies.Assembly object>, print_info=False)

cns.data_utils module

Utility functions for loading and managing copy number segment (CNS) data.

This module provides functions to load, filter, and manage CNS data from different sources (PCAWG, TRACERx, TCGA). It includes utilities for handling samples, loading binned data, and managing file paths.

cns.data_utils.get_cns_for_type(cns, samples, type)

Extract CNS data for a specific cancer type.

Parameters:

  • cns (pd.DataFrame) – CNS data.

  • samples (pd.DataFrame) – Sample information.

  • type (str) – Cancer type to extract.

Returns:

CNS data filtered for the specified cancer type.

Return type:

pd.DataFrame

cns.data_utils.get_root_path()

Get the root path of the CNSistent package.

Returns:

Absolute path to the package root directory.

Return type:

str

cns.data_utils.load_COSMIC()

Load COSMIC consensus genes.

Returns:

DataFrame containing COSMIC gene data.

Return type:

pd.DataFrame

cns.data_utils.load_ENSEMBL()

Load ENSEMBL coding genes.

Returns:

DataFrame containing ENSEMBL gene data.

Return type:

pd.DataFrame

cns.data_utils.load_all_samples(filter=True, retype=True, print_info=False)

Load samples from all available datasets.

Parameters:

  • filter (bool, optional) – Whether to filter samples based on coverage and aneuploidy.

  • retype (bool, optional) – Whether to standardize cancer type labels.

  • print_info (bool, optional) – Whether to print progress information.

Returns:

Dictionary containing DataFrames for each dataset.

Return type:

dict

cns.data_utils.load_cns_out(filename, raw=False)

Load CNS data from the output directory.

Parameters:

  • filename (str) – Name of the file to load from the output directory.

  • raw (bool, optional) – If True, return the raw DataFrame without renaming columns.

Returns:

DataFrame containing CNS data.

Return type:

pd.DataFrame

cns.data_utils.load_merged_bins(select_samples, segment_size)

Load and merge binned CNS data.

Parameters:

  • select_samples (pd.DataFrame) – Samples to include.

  • segment_size (int) – Size of the bins.

Returns:

Combined binned CNS data.

Return type:

pd.DataFrame

cns.data_utils.load_merged_cns(select_samples=None)

Load and merge CNS data from all datasets.

Parameters:

select_samples (pd.DataFrame, optional) – DataFrame containing sample information to filter by.

Returns:

Combined CNS data filtered by the selected samples.

Return type:

pd.DataFrame

cns.data_utils.load_merged_samples(filter=True, retype=True, print_info=False)

Load and merge samples from all datasets.

Parameters:

  • filter (bool, optional) – Whether to filter samples based on coverage and aneuploidy.

  • retype (bool, optional) – Whether to standardize cancer type labels.

  • print_info (bool, optional) – Whether to print progress information.

Returns:

Combined DataFrame containing all samples.

Return type:

pd.DataFrame

cns.data_utils.load_samples_out(filename)

Load sample data from the output directory.

Parameters:

filename (str) – Name of the sample file to load.

Returns:

DataFrame containing sample data.

Return type:

pd.DataFrame

cns.data_utils.main_load_data(segment_type=None)

Main function to load both samples and CNS data.

Parameters:

segment_type (int, optional) – If provided, load binned data with this segment size.

Returns:

(samples DataFrame, CNS DataFrame)

Return type:

tuple

cns.data_utils.save_cns_fig(fig_name, fig=None)

Save a figure to the images directory.

Parameters:

  • fig_name (str) – Name of the figure file (without extension).

  • fig (matplotlib.figure.Figure, optional) – Figure to save. If None, uses current figure.

cns.data_utils.save_doc_fig(fig_name, fig=None)

Save a figure to the documentation directory.

Parameters:

  • fig_name (str) – Name of the figure file (without extension).

  • fig (matplotlib.figure.Figure, optional) – Figure to save. If None, uses current figure.
